subject: Accounting
title: T-Accounts Made Simple
description: A short lesson to help you apply T-Accounts Made Simple.

hook.text: Draw a big letter T. Write debits on the left and credits on the right. You've just made a T-account.

buildup.text: T-accounts are a visual tool for tracking increases and decreases in individual accounts.

discovery.text: Each account gets its own T. Debits go left, credits go right. The balance is the difference.

twist.text: T-accounts are rarely used in practice anymore, but they remain the best way to learn the logic.

climax.text: Master T-accounts and double-entry becomes intuitive—you'll see debits and credits everywhere.

punchline.text: A simple T teaches the most powerful concept in accounting.

quiz.question: Where do debits go in a T-account?
